text.title: Figma AI Tool Catalog and Agent Boundary
text.summary: Figma AI tools catalog, the agent boundary rule, and what the REST and Webhooks APIs actually expose.

list.item: Figma Make — prompt-based web app prototype generation; UI-only, no API or CLI
Figma Draw — generative AI vector sketching; UI-only, no external API
Figma Sites — publish landing pages directly from Figma; publish is UI-only; published URLs are agent-auditable
AI Image Tools — erase objects, isolate objects, expand images (raster only); operates on canvas, no export/re-import cycle; UI-only

rule.statement: None of Figma's AI features (Make, Draw, Sites publishing, Image Tools) can be triggered by agents via any API — all require a human in a Figma UI session.
rule.rationale: There is no API or programmatic interface to these features. Any workflow that claims to "use Figma Make via an agent" requires a human at the keyboard. Agents that assume otherwise will silently fail.

rule.statement: Use Figma Webhooks instead of polling the REST API to detect file changes.
rule.rationale: Figma REST API rate limit is 150 requests/minute per token. Polling a complex file for changes quickly exhausts limits. Webhooks are event-driven and do not count against the rate limit for detection.

list.item: Figma REST API — read file structure, components, styles; limited write (update existing node properties only; cannot create new frames)
Figma Webhooks API — event-driven: file_update, library_publish, comment events; triggers agent audit passes

antipattern.description: Fetching the full file JSON for complex Figma files.
antipattern.reason: Figma file JSON can be 50–500 MB for complex files. Always request specific node subtrees using the ?ids=node_id parameter. Fetching the full file at scale will exhaust rate limits and memory.

antipattern.description: Using Figma Sites published URLs as canonical references without re-checking after file changes.
antipattern.reason: Figma Sites published URLs do not reflect file changes until a human manually re-publishes. Agents monitoring the URL may audit stale content if they assume the URL is always current.
